analysis_result: CDKN2A somatic point-mutation frequency in the 54-sample WES cohort = 0/54 = 0.0%. The MOESM5 MAF (FILTER==PASS) contains ZERO CDKN2A rows (of any Variant_Classification). The paper's reported 58% in the Fig 2b oncoprint therefore reflects predominantly CDKN2A CNV/homozygous deletions rather than somatic point mutations; CNV calls are not contained in the MOESM5 MAF and could not be integrated in this analysis (a CONUMEE/methylation-array substitute would be needed).
denominator_used: N = 54 WES samples (all rows in MOESM4 with WES=='X'; barcodes constructed as 'Tumor_' + WES label).
assumptions_made: Non-synonymous variant set = {Missense_Mutation, Nonsense_Mutation, Frame_Shift_Del, Frame_Shift_Ins, Splice_Site, In_Frame_Del, In_Frame_Ins, Translation_Start_Site, Nonstop_Mutation}; FILTER==PASS (all 7457 MOESM5 rows already pass this filter); WES cohort defined by MOESM4 'WES'=='X' yielding 54 samples; Tumor_Sample_Barcode constructed as 'Tumor_'+WES label (note: 5 expected barcodes are absent from the MAF and 3 MAF barcodes do not match an expected WES label, but this is irrelevant here because CDKN2A has 0 MAF rows entirely); CNV deletions of CDKN2A (a major contributor to the paper's 58% oncoprint bar) are not present in the MOESM5 MAF and were not available for integration.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - MOESM4 (sample metadata): 108 rows x 15 columns; 54 rows with WES=='X'.
   - MOESM5 (somatic MAF): 7457 rows x 123 columns; 52 unique Tumor_Sample_Barcode values.
   - Columns used: Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Hugo_Symbol, Variant_Classification, FILTER.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter 1: MOESM4['WES']=='X' -> 54 WES samples.
   - Expected barcodes = 'Tumor_' + WES label -> 54 unique barcodes.
   - Cross-check against MAF: 49 of 54 barcodes appear in the MAF; 5 expected barcodes are absent ({Tumor_2138, Tumor_M3398, Tumor_882, Tumor_2178, Tumor_2414}); 3 extra barcodes appear in the MAF ({Tumor_1869, Tumor_M2242, Tumor_RT3379}). Denominator for frequency = full 54-sample WES cohort per question.

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Hugo_Symbol == 'CDKN2A' (exact symbol).
   - FILTER == 'PASS' (all 7457 MAF rows satisfy this).
   - Variant_Classification IN non-synonymous set (see Assumptions).
   - Variant_Classification distribution in MAF: Missense_Mutation 4572, Silent 2390, Nonsense_Mutation 158, Frame_Shift_Del 120, Splice_Site 85, In_Frame_Del 43, In_Frame_Ins 39, Frame_Shift_Ins 37, Translation_Start_Site 7, Nonstop_Mutation 6.

D. COMPUTATION
   - Subsetting MOESM5 to (FILTER=='PASS') & (Hugo_Symbol=='CDKN2A') & (Variant_Classification in non-synonymous set) returned 0 rows.
   - Broader check: (Hugo_Symbol=='CDKN2A') with no filter also returned 0 rows; i.e., CDKN2A does not appear anywhere in the MAF.
   - Related loci present in MAF: CDKN2B n=1, CDK4 n=1, RB1 n=1. No CDKN2A entries at all.
   - Numerator = 0 samples with CDKN2A somatic point mutation.
   - Denominator = 54 WES samples.
   - Frequency = 0/54 = 0.0%.

E. RESULT
   - Somatic point mutation frequency of CDKN2A in the 54-sample WES cohort: 0.0% (0/54).
   - Paper's reported 58% for CDKN2A in Fig 2b cannot be reproduced from MAF alone because the MAF contains zero CDKN2A variants. The 58% bar in the Fig 2b oncoprint is therefore accounted for by CDKN2A copy-number deletions/homozygous losses (consistent with CDKN2A biology in MPNST, where biallelic inactivation is typically via CNV loss rather than SNV/indel).

F. CAVEATS
   - CNV calls for CDKN2A (which likely drive the paper's 58%) are not present in MOESM5 and were not integrated here. A CONUMEE analysis of the MethylationEPIC IDAT data would be required to reproduce the oncoprint bar.
   - Five of the 54 WES-labelled samples have no matching barcode in the MAF, and three MAF barcodes do not correspond to an entry in the 54-sample WES label list - the mismatch does not affect this result because CDKN2A has 0 MAF rows regardless of cohort restriction.
   - No non-PASS filter relaxation would change the answer (all MAF rows are PASS and none are CDKN2A).
   - The non-synonymous variant set used is the standard set excluding Silent/5'UTR/3'UTR/Intron; the MAF contains only coding classes so the choice of non-synonymous set does not affect this result.
